hyperedges.label: Per-command JSON DTOs forming the stable agent contract
Cross-backend fidelity for v1.2 features
hyperedges.rationale: Plan-level connection: Track 2 parity work ties together fixture-table sharing, Tekton-Reason mapping, ephemeral CM/Secret apply, and task-retry event mirroring so each v1.2 feature behaves identically under --docker and --cluster.

hyperedges.label: cluster.Backend.RunPipeline submit→watch→outcome flow
hyperedges.rationale: RunPipeline's pipeline: ensure namespace, apply ephemeral CM/Secret sources, build unstructured PR, submit, watch to terminal status, walk TaskRuns into outcomes — all glued together by run.go.

hyperedges.label: PipelineBackend opt-in contract
hyperedges.rationale: Engine routing inspects whether the configured backend implements PipelineBackend; if so it delegates the entire PipelineRun including invocation+result types — used by cluster.Backend, bypassed by docker.Backend.

hyperedges.label: cross-backend event-shape fidelity (docker live vs cluster post-hoc)
shared e2e fixture set drives both backend harnesses
Manager allocates workspaces, results, and volume scratch dirs

nodes.label: Stable JSON output contract for agents
nodes.norm_label: stable json output contract for agents

nodes.label: Docker mode vs Cluster mode (k3d) backends
nodes.norm_label: docker mode vs cluster mode (k3d) backends

nodes.label: Contribution rule: tests required for Go production changes
nodes.norm_label: contribution rule: tests required for go production changes

nodes.label: Pretty output for humans (live ordered step logs, color, verbosity)
nodes.norm_label: pretty output for humans (live ordered step logs, color, verbosity)

nodes.label: Track 1: Tekton-upstream feature parity
nodes.rationale: Ranked by frequency in real-world Tekton catalogs, not implementation ease — items at the top force users onto --cluster today

nodes.label: Track 2: Backend parity (docker vs cluster)
nodes.rationale: Closing parity gaps before adding new backends (kind, podman); 'fidelity' becomes a tested invariant rather than a claim

nodes.label: Task.sidecars docker-backend limitation
nodes.rationale: Catalog Tasks rely on database/mock-service sidecars; docker mode needs per-Task network + shared netns design work; cluster mode already works

nodes.label: PipelineRun-level timeouts feature
nodes.rationale: Wraps the per-Task timeout from v1.2; needs an outer policy loop and one new exit-code disambiguation

nodes.label: PipelineBackend opt-in interface design
nodes.rationale: Opt-in interface lets the engine delegate the whole pipeline to backends that want to drive the DAG themselves; cluster backend uses this so the real Tekton controller drives the DAG

nodes.label: Lazy cluster + Tekton provisioning on Prepare
nodes.rationale: First --cluster run lazily creates cluster, installs Tekton, runs pipeline, leaves cluster up for fast re-runs; cluster lifecycle invisible by default
nodes.norm_label: lazy cluster + tekton provisioning on prepare

nodes.label: Dynamic client + unstructured.Unstructured for Tekton CRDs
nodes.rationale: Tekton CRDs aren't in k8s.io/api; using dynamic client + unstructured avoids vendoring tektoncd/pipeline (per v1 spec rationale)
nodes.norm_label: dynamic client + unstructured.unstructured for tekton crds

nodes.label: kubectl apply shell-out for Tekton release install
nodes.rationale: Server-side apply via client-go for hundreds of resources is ~250 lines we don't want to write; shell-out keeps the spec compact
nodes.norm_label: kubectl apply shell-out for tekton release install

nodes.label: Idempotent Tekton installer (CRD presence check)
nodes.rationale: Skip apply if pipelines.tekton.dev CRD already exists; only apply on first run to avoid redundant kubectl traffic

nodes.label: Known gap: no per-task EvtTaskStart/EvtTaskEnd in v1.1
nodes.rationale: runViaPipelineBackend only emits run-start and run-end; user sees logs streamed by step but not the tree-status output the Docker backend produces; deferred to follow-up
nodes.norm_label: known gap: no per-task evttaskstart/evttaskend in v1.1

nodes.label: Shared fixture descriptor across backends
nodes.rationale: Same fixture table runs through both docker and cluster harnesses; divergences become explicit DockerOnly/ClusterOnly flags rather than silent omissions; only descriptor list is shared, helpers stay in their own files

nodes.label: Tekton terminal-condition Reason mapping
nodes.rationale: Tekton sets Reason: PipelineRunTimeout/TaskRunTimeout distinctly; mapping to status 'timeout' makes parity tests pass — without mapping a timed-out PipelineRun on cluster reports 'failed' while docker reports 'timeout' for same input

nodes.label: Ephemeral ConfigMap/Secret apply before submit
nodes.rationale: Cluster backend reads bytes from same volumes.Store the docker backend uses, then kubectl-applies an ephemeral ConfigMap/Secret into the per-run namespace; without this, volumes/ fixture cannot pass under --cluster

nodes.label: task-retry event shape parity across backends
nodes.rationale: Cluster backend delegates to Tekton's retry impl but JSON event stream must match docker's policy loop; agents shouldn't have to special-case which backend they're talking to
nodes.norm_label: task-retry event shape parity across backends

nodes.label: Persona B: Tekton-fidelity differentiator
nodes.rationale: Catalog/platform engineer gets real Tekton reconciliation loop, real CRDs, real entrypoint shim, real result-files semantics, real $(context.pipelineRun.uid), real volumeClaimTemplate workspace lifecycle

nodes.label: Per-run namespace tkn-act-<runid8>
nodes.rationale: Multiple invocations don't collide; cleanup NOT done automatically — post-mortem inspection is the whole point of the cluster backend; v1.2 will add cluster prune

nodes.label: volumeClaimTemplate workspace strategy
nodes.rationale: Auto-allocated workspaces use volumeClaimTemplate with default storage class; user-supplied -w name=./path prints warning and ignored — v1.1 doesn't bind-mount host paths into k3d nodes

nodes.label: Per-step container log streaming strategy
nodes.rationale: One TaskRun = one pod; steps are containers named step-<name>; Tekton's entrypoint shim ensures only one container is active at a time so log streams don't interleave confusingly

nodes.label: Tekton release pinning to v0.65.0
nodes.rationale: Pinning means bumping requires a smoke test + CHANGELOG entry; risk: divergent kubectl versions parse release.yaml slightly differently — mitigation is tested kubectl + Tekton pair

nodes.label: AGENTS.md as canonical doc + embedded in binary
nodes.rationale: Single canonical document for AI agents; embedded via go:embed and printed by tkn-act agent-guide so agents that don't have repo access can still read it
nodes.norm_label: agents.md as canonical doc + embedded in binary

nodes.label: tkn-act doctor preflight diagnostic
nodes.rationale: Single command an AI agent should run first to determine if environment is ready and which optional features are available; exit 0 if all required checks pass, exit 3 (environment) otherwise

nodes.label: tkn-act help-json mechanical introspection
nodes.rationale: Walks the cobra command tree and emits structured document; lets an agent build a correct invocation without parsing --help output

nodes.label: Stable typed exit-code contract
nodes.rationale: Typed cliError{code, err} flows through RunE; main extracts the code; defaults to 1 if a plain error escapes — public contract safe to branch on

nodes.label: Phase 1: engine policy loop (onError, retries, timeout)
nodes.rationale: Wraps existing runOne in a policy loop: timeout never retries, succeeded/skipped break out, otherwise emit task-retry; final emit task-end with attempts count
nodes.norm_label: phase 1: engine policy loop (onerror, retries, timeout)

nodes.label: Phase 2: per-step results dirs
nodes.rationale: Each step gets RW /tekton/steps/<this-step>/results plus RO mounts for every earlier step in the same Task; resolver substitutes $(step.results.<name>.path) and $(steps.<earlier>.results.<name>)

nodes.label: Phase 3: local-friendly volumes (4 kinds)
nodes.rationale: Only emptyDir/hostPath/configMap/secret supported in docker mode; kubernetes-y kinds (csi/projected/PVC/downwardAPI) explicitly rejected by validator with exit 4

nodes.label: Agent-contract: additive-only changes for v1.2
nodes.rationale: New task-retry event, new timeout status, new exit code 6; no existing field renamed or type-changed; help-json picks up new flags automatically

nodes.label: tests-required CI gate
nodes.rationale: Every Go code change ships with a test change; override with [skip-test-check] token in commit message for genuinely test-immune changes

nodes.label: PR-only path-scoped workflow gates
nodes.rationale: Pushes to main or release/** ignore the path filter and always run both, so a release branch can never ship without the full matrix going green

nodes.label: TaskOutcomeOnCluster (cluster-side per-task summary)
nodes.rationale: Carries Attempts and RetryAttempts so engine can emit task-retry/task-end events shape-equivalent to docker side

nodes.label: cluster.Options (with ConfigMaps/Secrets stores)
nodes.rationale: ConfigMaps/Secrets back the per-Task volumes the docker side resolves locally; nil stores mean 'no volume sources configured' and a fixture that references one will fail at submit time, mirroring docker behavior

nodes.label: Backend.RunTask (returns 'not supported')
nodes.rationale: Cluster backend prefers RunPipeline; per-task RunTask intentionally rejects so engine routes via PipelineBackend interface

nodes.label: mapPipelineRunStatus (Reason→status table)
nodes.rationale: Keeps the table next to the cluster watch so docker engine.RunResult.Status and cluster engine.RunResult.Status emit the same value for the same outcome

nodes.label: Backend.applyVolumeSources (cluster pre-submit hook)
nodes.rationale: Mirrors docker side: if a volume references a source the store can't resolve, bubbles up here as a hard error before submit

nodes.label: engine cluster events tests (retries shape match)
nodes.norm_label: engine cluster events tests (retries shape match)
